revision.comment: Bot: mark 1 deleted QID(s) as DELETED_QID in ill templates [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/24376438097|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:古津賀古墳]] {{Infobox ancient site |name = Kotsuka Kofun |image =
Mound overview (stone chamber entrance at center) |alternate_name = |part_of = |location = {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|en|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|qid=Q166946|lt=Shimanto|1=Shimanto, Kōchi (city)}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|qid=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Kotsuka
(Within the grounds of Kotsuka Shrine) |coordinates = {{Coord|32.984175|N|132.956775|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}|omitted = JP-39 |type = {{ill|Round Kofun|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} |area = Diameter: 13 m |built = Late {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}} |burial = Two-sleeved {{ill|stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} (wooden coffin inside) |Interred_individual = |burial goods = Gold rings, iron sword, belt decorations, iron arrowheads, knives, and more |alternate_name = Shimanto City Designated Historic Site: Kotsuka Kofun |notes = |map = Japan Kochi |map_dot_label = Kotsuka Kofun |type = Round mound |map_dot_label = top | image = Kotsuka_Kofun,_zenkei.jpg |architectural_styles = {{ill|circular kofun|qid=Q11394747|1=Round Kofun}} }} '''Kotsuka Kofun''' (古津賀古墳) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|en|Shimanto, Kōchi (city)|qid=Q166946|lt=Shimanto|1=Shimanto, Kōchi (city)}}, {{ill|Kōchi Prefecture|en|Kōchi Prefecture|qid=Q134093|lt=Kochi Prefecture|1=Kōchi Prefecture}}, Japan. The mound is {{ill|Round Kofun|lt=round-shaped|ja|円墳|1=Round Kofun|1=Round Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Round Kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} and has been designation1 as a Shimanto City Historic Site. == Overview == Situated in western Kochi Prefecture, the Kofun is built on the slopes of a small hill (Mine-no-Mori) east of the former Nakamura city center. The name "Kotsuka" originates from "Kozuka" (old mound), and the nearby {{ill|Kotsuka Shrine|ja|古津賀神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuka Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=DELETED_QID|1=Q135504173}} is said to have been founded as a site of worship for this kofun.{{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=古津賀古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409}} Archaeological excavations were conducted in {{ill|1962|en|1962|qid=Q2764|lt=1962|1=1962}} and {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}} (Showa 37 and 48). The mound is elliptical in type with a diameter of approximately 13 meters. The burial facility consists of a two-sleeved {{ill|stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} that opens southeastward. The total chamber length (corridor and main chamber) is 7.7 meters, making it the largest stone chamber in western Kochi Prefecture. The chamber is constructed using siliceous sandstone, {{ill|Sandstone|en|Sandstone|qid=Q13085|lt=sandstone|1=Sandstone}}, and {{ill|Quartzite|en|Quartzite|qid=Q237883|lt=quartzite|1=Quartzite}}, likely sourced from the Tanogawa area in the midstream of the {{ill|Gogawa River|ja|後川|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gogawa River|1=Ushirogawa River|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q16766577}}. The chamber floor is lined with river stones collected from the Gogawa basin, and a wooden coffin was placed on top of these stones. burial goods recovered from the chamber include burial goods, as described below. The Kotsuka Kofun is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}}, in the latter half of the {{ill|6th century|en|6th century|qid=Q8090|lt=6th century|1=6th century}}. The scarcity of kofun in the Hata region highlights the significance of this site. The area is also recognized as the {{ill|Kotsuka Archaeological Site|ja|古津賀遺跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotsuka Archaeological Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, where ritual burial goods related to river worship have been found in the floodplain of the Gogawa River.[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/10282 『古津賀遺跡群 第1次-第6次発掘調査報告（四万十市文化財調査報告 第1輯）』] Shimanto City Board of Education, 2006. In {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} (Showa 39), the kofun site was designation1 as a historic site by the former Nakamura city government (now Shimanto City). == Excavation History == * Edo period: The mound was a site of worship, as evidenced by the discovery of {{ill|Kan'ei Tsūhō|en|Kan'ei Tsūhō|qid=Q11457504|lt=Kan'ei Tsūhō|1=Kan'ei Tsūhō}} coins and Haji pottery during investigations. * {{ill|1962|en|1962|qid=Q2764|lt=1962|1=1962}}: First excavation. * {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}}: Designated as a historic site by Nakamura City. * {{ill|1973|en|1973|qid=Q2477|lt=1973|1=1973}}: Second excavation; first restoration. * {{ill|1974|en|1974|qid=Q2478|lt=1974|1=1974}}: Second restoration. File:Kotsuka Kofun, sendou.jpg|Interior of the stone chamber
{{small|View from the corridor toward the main chamber.}} File:Kotsuka Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance of the stone chamber File:Kotsuka-jinja, shaden.jpg|Kotsuka Shrine
{{small|The mound is located behind the shrine.}} File:古津賀遺跡 出土品.JPG|burial goods from the Kotsuka Archaeological Site
{{small|Exhibited at the Kochi Prefectural Museum of History.}} == burial goods == Significant burial goods excavated include the following:{{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2009|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/10247 高知県後期古墳資料集I（高知大学考古学調査研究報告 第7冊）]|publisher=Kochi University Archaeology Research Lab}} * Two gold rings * One iron sword * One belt decoration * Over 15 iron arrowheads * More than three knives The burial goods are preserved and displayed at the Shimanto Municipal Museum of Local History (Shimanto City, Nakamura). == Cultural Property == === Shimanto City Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ''' Kotsuka Kofun - Designated in {{ill|1964|en|1964|qid=Q2652|lt=1964|1=1964}} (Showa 39).[http://www.city.shimanto.lg.jp/kyouiku/bunkazai/kinen.html List of Historic Site] (Shimanto City website).. == Related Facilities == * Shimanto Municipal Museum of Local History (Shimanto City, Nakamura) - Houses and exhibits burial goods from Kotsuka Kofun. == References == {{Reflist}} == Further Reading == * Historical Information Panel (Installed by Shimanto City Board of Education) * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=1983|chapter=古津賀古墳|title=[[日本歴史地名大系]] 40 高知県の地名|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490409}} * {{Cite book|editor=|author=|year=2009|chapter=|title=[https://sitereports.nabunken.go.jp/10247 高知県後期古墳資料集I（高知大学考古学調査研究報告 第7冊）]|publisher=Kochi University Archaeology Research Lab}} == External Links == * {{Commonscat-inline|Kotsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] [[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]] [[Template:infobox ancient site]] [[File:Kotsuka Kofun, zenkei.jpg|280px]] {{translated page|ja|古津賀古墳|version=99995520|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:古津賀古墳]] {{Commons category|Kotsuka Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q55522042}} [[Category:Kofun of Kochi prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal Historic Sites in Kōchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Municipal History of Shimanto]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Round Kofun]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages with deleted QID in ill template]]
